events.text: Melissa Scott, 63-year-old Black female, Current smoker (1 ppd). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with High-grade serous carcinoma of the Right ovary. Clinical staging: T3cN0M0, AJCC Stage IIIC. ICD-10: C56.9. Stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian cancer with BRCA1 mutation.
PET/CT performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Underwent primary debulking surgery: Total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, omentectomy, and optimal cytoreduction (residual disease < 1 cm). Estimated blood loss 400 mL. Length of stay 4 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 2 anemia. Hemoglobin 8.5 g/dL. Transfused 2 units pRBC. Iron studies checked.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 neuropathy. Dose reduction of neurotoxic agent by 25%. Gabapentin 300 mg TID started.
PET/CT performed for restaging after first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic.
Initiated second-line platinum-based chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1,8 q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated second-line platinum-based chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1,8 q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 2 neuropathy. Dose reduction of neurotoxic agent by 25%. Gabapentin 300 mg TID started.
Initiated second-line platinum-based chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1,8 q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated second-line platinum-based chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1,8 q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after second-line platinum-based chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after second-line platinum-based chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.

documents.text:
PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Melissa Scott
MRN: patient_cf85d5603332
Date of Study: 2022-03-16

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Complex cystic and solid mass in the right adnexa measuring 6.8 cm. Omental caking present. Moderate ascites. Peritoneal carcinomatosis.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Complex adnexal mass with peritoneal carcinomatosis and ascites, concerning for advanced ovarian malignancy.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Melissa Scott
MRN: patient_cf85d5603332
Specimen ID: S2022-84203
Date of Procedure: 2022-03-19
Date of Report: 2022-03-22

SPECIMEN: Right ovary, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade serous carcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.9 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
WT1: Positive
P53: Aberrant (overexpression)
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Melissa Scott - Right ovary" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 6.9 x 4.8 x 3.5 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A9.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Gynecologic Oncology

Patient: Melissa Scott
MRN: patient_cf85d5603332
Date: 2022-03-20
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Scott is a 63-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary, IIIC. Diagnosis made following evaluation of progressive symptoms. Stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian cancer with BRCA1 mutation. Pathology confirmed high-grade serous carcinoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports abdominal discomfort and early satiety.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- coronary artery disease
- COPD

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Cesarean section x 2 (if applicable)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Never smoker
- Alcohol: Former heavy drinker, quit 5 years ago
- Occupation: Accountant
- Lives with spouse and adult children

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with prostate cancer at age 68. Paternal uncle with lung cancer.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: joint pain, mild dyspnea on exertion. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 148/72, HR 85, RR 17, Temp 97.7F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 58.8 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 6.7, Hgb 10.7, Plt 337, Cr 1.1

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
63-year-old female with IIIC high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. initial oncology consultation.

1. High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary, Stage IIIC
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend primary debulking surgery
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Carlos Mendez, MD
Gynecologic Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Melissa Scott
MRN: patient_cf85d5603332
Date of Report: 2022-03-31
Specimen: Right ovary (Biopsy)
Diagnosis: High-grade serous carcinoma
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 8.0 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRCA1 p.E1836fs (c.5503delA) - Frameshift (Exon 22) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  NF1 p.R1947* - Nonsense (Exon 39)
  TP53 p.C176Y - Missense (Exon 5)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CDK12 p.T826A - VUS
  ARID1A p.D1850N - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA1 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitors include olaparib, niraparib, rucaparib.
  - Platinum sensitivity expected.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 794x coverage.
Tumor content: 63%.

Electronically signed by: Andrew Patel, MD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Melissa Scott
MRN: patient_cf85d5603332
Specimen ID: S2022-34364
Date of Procedure: 2022-04-20
Date of Report: 2022-04-23

SPECIMEN: Right ovary, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade serous carcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 3.4 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
WT1: Positive
P53: Aberrant (overexpression)
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Melissa Scott - Right ovary" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 3.4 x 2.4 x 1.7 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A9.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Medical Oncology

Patient: Melissa Scott
MRN: patient_cf85d5603332
Date: 2022-04-25
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: postoperative follow-up

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Scott is a 63-year-old female with IIIC high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary who presents for postoperative follow-up. Patient has some concerns about side effects. Reports mild nausea but eating adequately.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: intermittent headaches, mild peripheral edema. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 113/67, HR 82, RR 14, Temp 98.1F, SpO2 99% on RA, Wt 89.1 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 4.0, Hgb 12.8, Plt 169, Cr 0.78

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
63-year-old female with IIIC high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. postoperative follow-up.

1. High-grade serous carcinoma - postoperative follow-up
   - Continue current treatment regimen
   - Will check labs prior to next cycle
2. Symptom management
   - Neuropathy being monitored
3. Follow-up in 3 weeks

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD
Medical Oncology

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Melissa Scott
MRN: patient_cf85d5603332
Date of Study: 2023-09-23

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 3 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Complex cystic and solid mass in the right adnexa measuring 8.1 cm. Omental caking present. Moderate ascites. Peritoneal carcinomatosis.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Complex adnexal mass with peritoneal carcinomatosis and ascites, concerning for advanced ovarian malignancy.
2. Overall assessment: Partial response per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology
